items.title: Input, hidden, and output layers
items.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  i1["x1 input"] --> h1["h1 hidden"]
  i2["x2 input"] --> h1
  i3["x3 input"] --> h1
  i4["x4 input"] --> h1
  i1 --> h2["h2 hidden"]
  i2 --> h2
  i3 --> h2
  i4 --> h2
  i1 --> h3["h3 hidden"]
  i2 --> h3
  i3 --> h3
  i4 --> h3
  h1 --> o1["y1 output"]
  h2 --> o1
  h3 --> o1
  h1 --> o2["y2 output"]
  h2 --> o2
  h3 --> o2
items.caption: A fully connected feedforward network: each neuron in one layer connects to every neuron in the next. Width is the neuron count per box; this example has depth 1 (one hidden layer).

items.title: Neuron compute unit
items.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  x1["x1"] --> sumNode["weighted sum + b"]
  x2["x2"] --> sumNode
  x3["x3"] --> sumNode
  w1["w1"] -.-> sumNode
  w2["w2"] -.-> sumNode
  w3["w3"] -.-> sumNode
  sumNode --> actFn["activation f"]
  actFn --> outA["activation a"]
items.caption: One neuron: linear combination of inputs with weights and bias, then a nonlinear activation. A layer runs this unit in parallel for each of its neurons.

items.title: Depth vs width in a two-hidden-layer MLP
items.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  inp["Input w=784"] --> h1["Hidden 1 w=256"]
  h1 --> h2["Hidden 2 w=128"]
  h2 --> outL["Output w=10"]
  noteDepth["depth = 2 hidden layers"] -.-> h1
  noteDepth -.-> h2
  noteWidth["width = neurons in that layer"] -.-> h1
items.caption: Depth counts stacked hidden layers (here 2). Width labels the neuron count at each stage (256 and 128 in the hidden tiers).
